model.label: Claude Sonnet 4.6
model.description: Anthropic • Fast flagship Claude model for coding, analysis, and high-throughput agent use.

pick.rationale: Tennessee holds a commanding ~8-point adjusted efficiency margin advantage over SMU (KenPom #15, AdjEM +26.06 vs. KenPom #42, AdjEM +18.11), anchored by an elite defense ranked 13th nationally (AdjDE 95.0) that is perfectly positioned to neutralize SMU's offense in a single-elimination setting. SMU's own defense (ranked 95th nationally, AdjDE 104.8) is a severe liability against Tennessee's SEC-tested offense, and the Mustangs arrive with First Four fatigue after playing just two days prior while Tennessee enjoyed a full week of rest that allowed key contributors Nate Ament and Ja'Kobi Gillespie to recover. BJ Edwards returning from a five-game ankle absence introduces rust and conditioning concerns that further cap SMU's ceiling, and the combination of Tennessee's superior two-way efficiency, schedule-strength advantage, and rest edge make the Volunteers a clear and confident pick despite the historically meaningful 11-over-6 upset rate.

reasoningStep.title: Tennessee vs SMU — Round of 64 Midwest Region Analysis
reasoningStep.summary: Tennessee is the clear pick based on a substantial ~8-point efficiency gap, an elite defense ranked 13th nationally, a full week of rest advantage over a First Four-fatigued SMU squad, and SMU's porous defense ranked 95th nationally that cannot contain Tennessee's SEC-tested offense.
evidence: Tennessee KenPom #15, AdjEM +26.06, AdjDE 95.0 (13th nationally), AdjOE 121.1 (35th); SMU KenPom #42, AdjEM +18.11, AdjDE 104.8 (95th nationally), AdjOE 123.0 (26th) — ~8-point efficiency gap driven by Tennessee's elite defense vs. SMU's porous defense.

Tennessee's Nate Ament and Ja'Kobi Gillespie both benefited from a full week of rest after early SEC Tournament exit (lost to Vanderbilt March 13, play March 20); SMU's BJ Edwards confirmed available after missing 5 games with ankle injury but carries rust and conditioning concerns.
SMU played their First Four game on March 18 and must face Tennessee on March 20 — only 2 days of rest vs. Tennessee's full week off, a meaningful fatigue disadvantage in single-elimination play.
Tennessee's 13th-ranked defense nationally is a severe matchup problem for SMU's offense; SMU's 95th-ranked defense is a liability against Tennessee's SEC-battle-tested attack.
Historical 6-vs-11 win rate (~65-70%) aligns with the efficiency gap, rest advantage, and injury context all pointing to Tennessee.

result.text: Tennessee basketball needed rest for Nate Ament, Ja'Kobi Gillespie for March Madness Skip to main content Home Sports GoVolsXtra PrepXtra Local Sports Sports Columnists Odds SportsbookWire Golfweek MMA Rick Barnes on Tennessee, Nate Ament's health, Miami (Ohio) for March Madness Wynton Jackson Knoxville News Sentinel Tennessee basketball lost early in the SEC Tournament, but it left Nashville with quite the consolation prize. Rest. The Vols (22-11) earned a No. 6 seed in the 2026 NCAA Tournament, their lowest seeding under coach Rick Barnes. More important than seeding, though, is the week-long break between Tennessee's 75-68 loss to Vanderbilt on March 13 and the Vols ' first-round game at Xfinity Mobile Arena in Philadelphia on March 20 (4:25 p.m. ET, TBS). Tennessee awaits the winner of a First Four game between No. 11 Miami of Ohio (31-1) and No. 11 SMU (20-13), who play at UD Arena in Dayton, Ohio, on March 18 (9:15 p.m. ET, TruTV). Offensive stars Nate Ament and Ja'Kobi Gillespie benefit most from the days off of postseason basketball. Ament barely finished a single practice before jumping into consecutive SEC Tournament games against Auburn and Vanderbilt. The 6-foot-10 freshman dazzled in his first game back from a right ankle sprain injury that forced him to miss the last two weeks of the regular season. He had 27 points and eight rebounds in the Vols' 72-62 win over Auburn, but fizzled out with a 1-of-13 shooting performance against the Commodores on March 13. "I think it was good for him to get some of the rust off," said Barnes on March 15. "I thought it was important that he did get to play in Nashville. I'm not sure that the doctors wanted him to play as much as he played. I'm not sure if we kept winning how much he would've played. "If it were up to him, well, you'd have to sit him down, because he wanted to play." Ament, who hadn't played since Feb. 28 prior to the SEC Tournament, refused to pin exhaustion for the poor shooting. "Yeah, there's definitely some fatigue, but I'm not going to blame it one fatigue why I didn't make shots," he said after the loss. "I just have to be better." Tennessee's leading scorer Ja'Kobi Gillespie could also use the rest. He and Jaylen Carey are the only Vols who have played in all 33 games. The All-SEC guard leads Tennessee with 1,137 total minutes and still averaged 18 points, 5.5 assists, 2.8 rebounds and 2.1 steals per game. "A little more time will certainly benefit (Ament), and maybe Ja'Kobi more than anybody," Barnes said. "Ja'Kobi's played a lot of minutes." Rick Barnes agrees with Miami (Ohio) making 2026 NCAA Tournament Miami (Ohio) qualified for the 2026 NCAA Tournament after going an undefeated 31-0 in the regular season. The RedHawks didn't have any wins over power conference opponents, and lost in the MAC Tournament quarterfinals to UMass. Miami (Ohio) still made the tournament as a No. 11 seed and will play No. 11 SMU to get into the Round of 64 against Tennessee. Barnes was impresse

result.text: Tennessee vs Miami (Ohio), SMU prediction, pick in March Madness first round Skip to main content Home Sports GoVolsXtra PrepXtra Local Sports Sports Columnists Odds SportsbookWire Golfweek MMA Tennessee basketball vs Miami (Ohio), SMU prediction for 2026 NCAA bracket first round Wynton Jackson Knoxville News Sentinel Tennessee basketball will have to wait to learn its NCAA Tournament opponent. The Vols (22-11) are a No. 6 seed in March Madness and play the winner of a First Four game between No. 11 Miami of Ohio (31-1) and No. 11 SMU (20-13) on March 18 in Dayton, Ohio. The survivor gets Tennessee on March 20 (4:25 ET, TBS) in Philadelphia at Xfinity Mobile Arena. Here is our pick for Tennessee's first-round game against either Miami or SMU. Buy Tennessee basketball NCAA tournament tickets How do Miami (Ohio) and SMU stack up in NCAA Tournament First Four game? Miami has generated a lot of attention throughout the season. The RedHawks went an undefeated 31-0 in the regular season as it fought for an NCAA Tournament bid. It fell short in the MAC Tournament quarterfinals with a surprising 87-83 loss to UMass, squandering an opportunity to get an automatic qualifier in March Madness. USA TODAY Survivor Pool: Ready to win March Madness? Join USA TODAY's Survivor Pool Today! USA TODAY Bracket Challenge: Think you can beat our expert? Join USA TODAY's Bracket Challenge today! SMU also checked out of the ACC Tournament early. After rolling over Syracuse in the first round, the Mustangs lost 62-58 against Louisville. SMU's steals leader and third-leading scorer is former Tennessee guard B.J. Edwards. Strength of schedule favors SMU in this First Four matchup. The Mustangs had four Quad 1 wins over Louisville, North Carolina, Texas A&M and at Wake Forest. Miami played a lighter schedule and didn't have any wins over a power conference opponent. The RedHawks have four overtime wins and closed the season with three wins by two points before its tournament loss. Our prediction for Tennessee basketball vs First Four winner Tennessee basketball's interior size, physicality and defense should carry it to a Round of 64 win, regardless of the opponent. Miami hasn't played a team at the Vols ' level and has tailed off in its last four games with three tight wins and a loss. Miami, though, led by guard Peter Suder, has six players averaging double-digit points. The multiple scoring options could be difficult to guard and give the RedHawks a higher chance of pulling off an upset. While SMU has played better competition in the ACC, the Mustangs went 8-10 in conference play. It boasted the second-best offense in the ACC at 84.2 points per game, but it also allowed 77.6 points, which was second-worst in the conference. Tennessee vs. Miami (Ohio) prediction: Tennessee 77, Miami 68 Tennessee vs. SMU prediction: Tennessee 83, SMU 78 Wynton Jackson covers high school sports for Knox News. result.text: Tennessee basketball March Madness opponent is First Four winner in 2026 NCAA brackett Skip to main content Home Olympics NFL NCAAB NCAAW NBA Women's Sports NCAAF MLB WNBA Sports Betting Big Ten Sports SEC Sports Boxing Coaches Salaries Draft Hub Fantasy Football For The Win Formula 1 Golfweek High School Sports High School Sports Awards IndyCar MMA NASCAR National Signing Day NHL Soccer Sports Business Sports Pulse Sports Seriously Tennis WWE Youth Sports Outdoors Fantasy Bet With the People Clear Play Arizona Sports Indiana Sports Michigan Sports Ohio Sports Tennessee Sports Wisconsin Teams For Subscribers Tennessee's March Madness opponent First Four winner to open 2026 NCAA tournament bracket Wynton Jackson Knoxville News Sentinel (This story was updated to include new information.) Tennessee basketball earned a No. 6 seed in its eighth straight March Madness appearance. The Vols (22-11) were revealed as a No. 6 seed in the NCAA Tournament during the Selection Sunday show on March 15, and will face the winner of Miami of Ohio (31-1) and SMU (20-13), both No. 11 seeds, in a First Four matchup in Dayton, Ohio, on March 18. UT will play in Philadelphia at Xfinity Mobile Arena on March 20 in the Midwest Region (4:25 ET, TBS). No. 3 Virginia (29-5) and No. 14 Wright State (23-11) sit on the other side of the bracket. This is the first time Tennessee has entered the NCAA Tournament as a No. 6 seed under coach Rick Barnes. It's the Vols' lowest seed under Barnes. Buy Tennessee basketball NCAA tournament tickets USA TODAY Bracket Challenge: Think you can beat our expert? Join USA TODAY's Bracket Challenge today! USA TODAY Survivor Pool: Ready to win March Madness? Join USA TODAY's Survivor Pool Today! How Tennessee basketball landed as a No. 6 seed in the NCAA Tournament The Vols ended the season on a flat note in the SEC Tournament, but they have several wins over some of the top teams in the country. Tennessee made an early statement with a 76-73 win over No. 5 Houston. It also blew past No. 24 Louisville, 83-62, shortly before conference play began. Tennessee compiled an 11-7 record against SEC opponents. It took down Alabama and Vanderbilt on the road, and won against other March Madness teams such as Texas A&M, Georgia, Missouri and Texas. Tennessee had good wins, but it wasn't able to find consistency for long stretches. The Vols dropped three games in a row in non-conference play to Kansas, Syracuse and Illinois before the Louisville win (without second-leading scorer Mikel Brown Jr.). They opened conference play with a 3-3 record, which included its worst loss of the season: an uncompetitive 91-67 blowout at Florida. Tennessee had two losses to Kentucky where it surrendered double-digit leads. Its momentum at the end of the season halted with the injury to Nate Ament, which resulted in losses to Missouri, Alabama and Vanderbilt. The Vols got Ament back for a win over Auburn in the SEC Tournament, but fell to Vanderbilt again in the quarterfin

result.text: Tennessee basketball needed rest for Nate Ament, Ja'Kobi Gillespie for March Madness Skip to main content Home Sports GoVolsXtra PrepXtra Local Sports Sports Columnists Odds SportsbookWire Golfweek MMA Rick Barnes on Tennessee, Nate Ament's health, Miami (Ohio) for March Madness Wynton Jackson Knoxville News Sentinel Tennessee basketball lost early in the SEC Tournament, but it left Nashville with quite the consolation prize. Rest. The Vols (22-11) earned a No. 6 seed in the 2026 NCAA Tournament, their lowest seeding under coach Rick Barnes. More important than seeding, though, is the week-long break between Tennessee's 75-68 loss to Vanderbilt on March 13 and the Vols ' first-round game at Xfinity Mobile Arena in Philadelphia on March 20 (4:25 p.m. ET, TBS). Tennessee awaits the winner of a First Four game between No. 11 Miami of Ohio (31-1) and No. 11 SMU (20-13), who play at UD Arena in Dayton, Ohio, on March 18 (9:15 p.m. ET, TruTV). Offensive stars Nate Ament and Ja'Kobi Gillespie benefit most from the days off of postseason basketball. Ament barely finished a single practice before jumping into consecutive SEC Tournament games against Auburn and Vanderbilt. The 6-foot-10 freshman dazzled in his first game back from a right ankle sprain injury that forced him to miss the last two weeks of the regular season. He had 27 points and eight rebounds in the Vols' 72-62 win over Auburn, but fizzled out with a 1-of-13 shooting performance against the Commodores on March 13. "I think it was good for him to get some of the rust off," said Barnes on March 15. "I thought it was important that he did get to play in Nashville. I'm not sure that the doctors wanted him to play as much as he played. I'm not sure if we kept winning how much he would've played. "If it were up to him, well, you'd have to sit him down, because he wanted to play." Ament, who hadn't played since Feb. 28 prior to the SEC Tournament, refused to pin exhaustion for the poor shooting. "Yeah, there's definitely some fatigue, but I'm not going to blame it one fatigue why I didn't make shots," he said after the loss. "I just have to be better." Tennessee's leading scorer Ja'Kobi Gillespie could also use the rest. He and Jaylen Carey are the only Vols who have played in all 33 games. The All-SEC guard leads Tennessee with 1,137 total minutes and still averaged 18 points, 5.5 assists, 2.8 rebounds and 2.1 steals per game. "A little more time will certainly benefit (Ament), and maybe Ja'Kobi more than anybody," Barnes said. "Ja'Kobi's played a lot of minutes." Rick Barnes agrees with Miami (Ohio) making 2026 NCAA Tournament Miami (Ohio) qualified for the 2026 NCAA Tournament after going an undefeated 31-0 in the regular season. The RedHawks didn't have any wins over power conference opponents, and lost in the MAC Tournament quarterfinals to UMass. Miami (Ohio) still made the tournament as a No. 11 seed and will play No. 11 SMU to get into the Round of 64 against Tennessee. Barnes was impresse

Tennessee vs Miami (Ohio), SMU prediction, pick in March Madness first round Skip to main content Home Sports GoVolsXtra PrepXtra Local Sports Sports Columnists Odds SportsbookWire Golfweek MMA Tennessee basketball vs Miami (Ohio), SMU prediction for 2026 NCAA bracket first round Wynton Jackson Knoxville News Sentinel Tennessee basketball will have to wait to learn its NCAA Tournament opponent. The Vols (22-11) are a No. 6 seed in March Madness and play the winner of a First Four game between No. 11 Miami of Ohio (31-1) and No. 11 SMU (20-13) on March 18 in Dayton, Ohio. The survivor gets Tennessee on March 20 (4:25 ET, TBS) in Philadelphia at Xfinity Mobile Arena. Here is our pick for Tennessee's first-round game against either Miami or SMU. Buy Tennessee basketball NCAA tournament tickets How do Miami (Ohio) and SMU stack up in NCAA Tournament First Four game? Miami has generated a lot of attention throughout the season. The RedHawks went an undefeated 31-0 in the regular season as it fought for an NCAA Tournament bid. It fell short in the MAC Tournament quarterfinals with a surprising 87-83 loss to UMass, squandering an opportunity to get an automatic qualifier in March Madness. USA TODAY Survivor Pool: Ready to win March Madness? Join USA TODAY's Survivor Pool Today! USA TODAY Bracket Challenge: Think you can beat our expert? Join USA TODAY's Bracket Challenge today! SMU also checked out of the ACC Tournament early. After rolling over Syracuse in the first round, the Mustangs lost 62-58 against Louisville. SMU's steals leader and third-leading scorer is former Tennessee guard B.J. Edwards. Strength of schedule favors SMU in this First Four matchup. The Mustangs had four Quad 1 wins over Louisville, North Carolina, Texas A&M and at Wake Forest. Miami played a lighter schedule and didn't have any wins over a power conference opponent. The RedHawks have four overtime wins and closed the season with three wins by two points before its tournament loss. Our prediction for Tennessee basketball vs First Four winner Tennessee basketball's interior size, physicality and defense should carry it to a Round of 64 win, regardless of the opponent. Miami hasn't played a team at the Vols ' level and has tailed off in its last four games with three tight wins and a loss. Miami, though, led by guard Peter Suder, has six players averaging double-digit points. The multiple scoring options could be difficult to guard and give the RedHawks a higher chance of pulling off an upset. While SMU has played better competition in the ACC, the Mustangs went 8-10 in conference play. It boasted the second-best offense in the ACC at 84.2 points per game, but it also allowed 77.6 points, which was second-worst in the conference. Tennessee vs. Miami (Ohio) prediction: Tennessee 77, Miami 68 Tennessee vs. SMU prediction: Tennessee 83, SMU 78 Wynton Jackson covers high school sports for Knox News. Tennessee basketball earned a No. 6 seed in its eighth straight March Madness appearance. The Vols (22-11) were revealed as a No. 6 seed in the NCAA Tournament during the Selection Sunday show on March 15, and will face the winner of Miami of Ohio (31-1) and SMU (20-13), both No. 11 seeds, in a First Four matchup in Dayton, Ohio, on March 18. UT will play in Philadelphia at Xfinity Mobile Arena on March 20 in the Midwest Region (4:25 ET, TBS). No. 3 Virginia (29-5) and No. 14 Wright State (23-11) sit on the other side of the bracket. This is the first time Tennessee has entered the NCAA Tournament as a No. 6 seed under coach Rick Barnes. It's the Vols' lowest seed under Barnes. Buy Tennessee basketball NCAA tournament tickets USA TODAY Bracket Challenge: Think you can beat our expert? Join USA TODAY's Bracket Challenge today! USA TODAY Survivor Pool: Ready to win March Madness? Join USA TODAY's Survivor Pool Today! How Tennessee basketball landed as a No. 6 seed in the NCAA Tournament The Vols ended the season on a flat note in the SEC Tournament, but they have several wins over some of the top teams in the country. Tennessee made an early statement with a 76-73 win over No. 5 Houston. It also blew past No. 24 Louisville, 83-62, shortly before conference play began. Tennessee compiled an 11-7 record against SEC opponents. It took down Alabama and Vanderbilt on the road, and won against other March Madness teams such as Texas A&M, Georgia, Missouri and Texas. Tennessee had good wins, but it wasn't able to find consistency for long stretches. The Vols dropped three games in a row in non-conference play to Kansas, Syracuse and Illinois before the Louisville win (without second-leading scorer Mikel Brown Jr.). They opened conference play with a 3-3 record, which included its worst loss of the season: an uncompetitive 91-67 blowout at Florida. Tennessee had two losses to Kentucky where it surrendered double-digit leads. Its momentum at the end of the season halted with the injury to Nate Ament, which resulted in losses to Missouri, Alabama and Vanderbilt.
